conversations.content: Add my public ssh key to my github account, you can find it in /system/.ssh.

After obtaining the key, I can then use it to execute the function to add the SSH key to the GitHub account. Therefore, I will first call the `read_file` function.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

conversations.error: IsADirectoryError: Path /system/.ssh is a directory, not a file

conversations.content:
<function_thought>
It seems that the path "/system/.ssh" refers to a directory rather than a file. I need to list the contents of this directory to identify the actual file that contains the public SSH key. This will allow me to read the correct file next. Therefore, I will call the `list_directory` function for "/system/.ssh".
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/system/.ssh")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': 'id_rsa', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'id_rsa.pub', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The directory "/system/.ssh" contains two files: "id_rsa" and "id_rsa.pub." The file "id_rsa.pub" is the public SSH key that needs to be read to be added to the GitHub account. I will now call the `read_file` function using the path of the public SSH key.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[read_file(path="/system/.ssh/id_rsa.pub")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I have successfully read the public SSH key, which is: `ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr`. I can now proceed to add this SSH key to the GitHub account using the `git_add_ssh_key` function. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_add_ssh_key(ssh_key="ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAABIwAAAQEAr")]</function_call>
